channel.title: CNBC International TV
channel.description:
CNBC is the recognized world leader in business news and provides real-time financial market coverage and business information to approximately 385 million homes worldwide.
With international headquarters in London and Singapore, CNBC International covers news and features from Europe, the Middle East, Africa and Asia Pacific.
Subscribe to CNBC International TV for the very best of CNBC's international programming, including top business news moments and award-winning original documentaries and feature series.
For real-time news updates from around the world, visit CNBC's website: http://www.cnbc.com
Subscribe to our other YouTube channel here: https://www.youtube.com/user/CNBCInternational/
Like our Facebook page www.facebook.com/cnbcinternational
Follow us on Twitter https://twitter.com/CNBCi

item.description: Viktor Shvets of Macquarie Capital says that's because inflation isn't arising from excess demand but from disruptions to the goods and services market as well as the labor market.
item.title: Monetary policy can't really address most of current inflationary pressures: Macquarie Capital
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Rebeca Minguela, founder and CEO of Clarity AI, says that's a challenge that applies not only to the climate crisis, but also to areas like diversity and other social issues.
item.title: Transparency is a 'huge problem' when it comes to sustainable investment, says Clarity AI CEO
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Qian Wang of Vanguard says some central banks in Asia's emerging markets are accelerating monetary policy tightening partly because of currency depreciation.
item.title: Currency depreciation is happening across Asia, says economist
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Michael Harris, founder of Cribstone Strategic Macro, discusses the prospect of the G-7 imposing a price cap on Russian oil exports.
item.title: Strategist says proposed price cap on Russian oil exports likely to benefit India and Turkey
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Dan Yergin of S&P Global discusses how inflation and the risk of a recession are affecting oil prices.
item.title: Dan Yergin explains why crude prices are falling
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Sweden Prime Minister Magdalena Andersson tells CNBC that Europe is going through a difficult economic situation. Meanwhile, Latvia's Prime Minister Arturs Karins says Ukrainians are paying with their lives and Europe with its wallet.
item.title: 'We're definitely in a difficult economic situation,' Sweden PM says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jim Townsend of the CNAS Transatlantic Security Program, who served as deputy assistant secretary of defense for Europe and NATO under the Obama administration, says Europe's view on China is more circumspect than what the U.S. might want it to be.
item.title: NATO sees Russia as a 'direct threat,' but not China, says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Reeve Collins, CEO of BlockV and a co-founder of Tether, explains the difference between algorithmic stablecoins and those that are backed by reserves and provides his outlook for crypto regulation.
item.title: Missteps and failures in crypto are inevitable, Tether co-founder says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Constantinos Herodotou, Governor of the Central Bank of Cyprus and Member of the ECB Governing Council, says inflation is likley to peak in the euro zone this year and shares his views on how the ECB should adapt its interest rate policy.
item.title: Bank of Cyprus governor says gradualism warranted as ECB normalizes monetary policy
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Creon Butler of Chatham House says China is unlikely to continue with its current level of spending as the country has been burnt in a number of ways by its debt issues.
item.title: China may not continue spending a large amount of money on its Belt and Road Initiative: Think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Trevor Greetham, head of multi-asset at Royal London Asset Management, discusses historical bear markets and says there could be a long, grinding period ahead for stock markets.
item.title: 'Don't get too sucked back in': Fund manager discusses what past bear markets can teach us
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, head of asset allocation research at Goldman Sachs, said high-growth tech companies are more vulnerable in the current macroeconomic environment.
item.title: We're still above the trough in tech, Goldman Sachs says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: David Loevinger of the TCW Group says the uncertainty will linger as long as the country's zero-Covid policy remains.
item.title: China's economic recovery is going to be 'very slow,' says asset management firm
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital has fallen into liquidation. The company is yet another victim of the plunge in cryptocurrency prices which has exposed its highly leveraged bets. CNBC's Arjun Kharpal reports on how 3AC got here.
item.title: Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital plunges into liquidation. This is how it happened
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Constantinos Herodotou, Governor of the Central Bank of Cyprus and Member of the ECB Governing Council, speaks to CNBC's Annette Weisbach about negotiations for a euro zone fragmentation tool, and lays out his inflation predictions.
item.title: ECB's Herodotou sees euro zone inflation peaking this year
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Shanta Devarajan of Georgetown University discusses Sri Lanka’s debt crisis.
item.title: Sri Lanka is on the verge of becoming a 'fragile state,' says professor
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Michael Howell, managing director at CrossBorder Capital, discusses the outlook for the euro/dollar currency pair and his views on so-called safe-haven assets as recession risks intensify.
item.title: Strategist says investors are quickly running out of safe assets as recession risks grow
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Gavin Chia of the wealth management platform discusses its expansion plans and how it educates clients about investments.
item.title: Futu Singapore says it is looking to expand in Southeast Asia
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jeffrey Schott of the Peterson Institute for International Economics says Europe is in a very tight situation and is playing for time.
item.title: G-7 has to prepare for a gas shutdown as energy crisis continues, says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tanah Sullivan of the company says there are serious blood, sweat and tears that have gone into helping it achieve its goals by 2030.
item.title: Indonesia's GoTo discusses what it needs to achieve its sustainability goals
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Marc Ostwald of ADM Investor Services International discusses the challenge that the ECB faces in balancing two very conflicting interests.
item.title: The European Central Bank started hiking rates too late, says futures brokerage company
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tan See Leng, Singapore's manpower minister, says the country's labor market continues to be tight but its long-term unemployment has dropped.
item.title: Long-term unemployment in Singapore is close to pre-Covid levels, says minister
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tina Fordham, founder of Fordham Global Foresight, discusses the overturning of Roe v. Wade and says there is disbelief on the part of U.S. allies over the reversal of what's seen as a fundamental right.
item.title: Reversal of Roe v. Wade is a 'business risk' for the United States, says geopolitical strategist
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank, weighs in on U.S. President Biden's gas tax plans, his outlook for oil prices, and what the commodities sector can tell us about a potential recession.
item.title: Commodities rout pointing to recession fears, says analyst
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ben van Beurden, CEO of the energy company, says an ever tighter oil and gas market is appearing.
item.title: Shell CEO says OPEC probably doesn't have as much spare capacity of oil as many people think
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Mohamad Al-Ississ, finance minister of Jordan, speaks exclusively to CNBC's Hadley Gamble.
item.title: Jordan finance minister: Jordan will be resilient to economic shocks
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jordan's King Abdullah II says he would be one of the first people that would endorse a Middle East NATO, but the vision of such an alliance must be very clear.
item.title: Jordan's king says he believes a Middle Eastern 'NATO' can be built with like-minded countries
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Chris Carey of Wells Fargo Securities says that while Pepsico can navigate and price consumer staples to their respective markets, strengthening of the U.S. dollar may affect multinational earnings, and increased retailer sensitivity to higher pricing requires close monitoring.
item.title: Retail pricing environment will evolve over the next few quarters: Wells Fargo
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Shares in UAE telco e& rose more than 5% on Monday after the company formerly known as Etisalat announced a major rebrand and strategic restructure.
item.title: Our aspiration is to accelerate growth, says e& CEO
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Neil Atkinson, independent oil analyst, discusses diplomatic relations between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia and how this is likely to influence energy markets.
item.title: Biden's visit to Saudi Arabia unlikely to result in a triumph for either party, oil analyst says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: CNBC's Silvia Amaro speaks to diplomats and policymakers at the European Council in Brussels, as fears grow about the region's future gas supplies from Russia.
item.title: Europe is growing increasingly worried about a total shutdown of Russian gas
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Alexander Treves of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses why that's the case even though the company is underweight on China A-shares.
item.title: JPMorgan explains why it likes Chinese tech stocks
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tan Teck Leng of UBS Global Wealth Management explains why it's a little bit worried about near-term sentiment.
item.title: UBS discusses the outlook for the dollar-yuan
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Cleveland Fed President Loretta Mester tells CNBC's Annette Weisbach about the likely outcome of the Federal Reserve's July meeting and gives her outlook on inflation and its impacts on the economy.
item.title: Fed's Mester says market volatility is probably here to stay
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: President of Kosovo Vjosa Osmani-Sadriu tells CNBC's Silvia Amaro why her country is pushing to join the EU, and how she perceives the bloc has dealt with the invasion of Ukraine.
item.title: President of Kosovo: We deserve to be in EU
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Alexander Irving, European transport analyst at AB Bernstein, discusses which airlines are best suited to weather the travel chaos and staff shortages plaguing a number of large airports across the region.
item.title: Bernstein analyst likes these two airlines despite travel chaos
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency, discusses clean energy, the climate crisis and price caps.
item.title: Emissions 'don't have a passport': IEA's Birol talks clean energy, climate change and price caps
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description:
Subscribe to CNBC International TV: http://cnb.cx/2wAkfMv
Subscribe to CNBC International: http://cnb.cx/2gft82z
Like our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/cnbcinternational
Follow us on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/cnbcinternational/
Follow us on Twitter https://twitter.com/cnbci
item.title: Full Interview: Presiden Indonesia Joko Widodo mengenai Warisan, G20, Elon Musk dan lain-lain
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Clemens Fuest, president of the IFO Institute, talks to CNBC's Julianna Tatelbaum.
item.title: IFO: Europe energy will face chllenges in winter when heating is needed
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Florian Ielpo, head of Macro and Multi-Asset Portfolio Manager at Lombard Odier Investment, discusses the rise in risk aversion in global markets.
item.title: Bad news is good news for markets right now, portfolio manager says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: In conversation with CNBC's Dan Murphy, Thomas Flohr, the founder and CEO of private jet subscription service VistaJet, discussed his company's carbon neutral commitment and why he doesn't think we'll see the electrification of the aviation industry anytime soon.
item.title: VistaJet chief: Electric planes are decades away
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Trita Parsi of The Quincy Institute says that when it comes to reasserting American leadership, there's likely to be more rhetoric than substance.
item.title: Biden's Middle East trip is 'quite questionable,' says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: VistaJet founder & CEO, Thomas Flohr welcomes CNBC's Dan Murphy on board one of the airline's latest Bombardier Global 7500 planes, explains how he disrupted a sector and shares his advice for getting ahead in business.
item.title: On board a Bombardier Global 7500 private jet
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Renee McGowan of Mercer discusses the results of its Cost of Living survey.
item.title: Housing in many Asian cities is expensive regardless of what the inflation rate is, says consultancy
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Charlie Robertson, author of The Time-Travelling Economist and global chief economist at Renaissance Capital, discusses the outlook for emerging markets.
item.title: I've put some of my own money into emerging markets, says economist
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ida Wolden Bache, governor at the Norges Bank, speaks to CNBC's Julianna Tatelbaum.
item.title: Norges Bank governor discusses Norway's biggest rate hike in 20 years
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Shrikant Chouhan of Kotak Securities says the multinational conglomerate is very aggressive in its digitalization efforts.
item.title: India's Reliance Industries is going to do well, says stock broking firm
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Rudiger von Fritsch of Berlin Global Advisors, formerly German ambassador to Russia, discusses the European Union's decision to grant Ukraine and Moldova candidate status.
item.title: Putin's ambitions go beyond occupying Ukrainian territory, says former German ambassador
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Michael Yoshikami, founder and CEO of Destination Wealth Management, says there's lots of uncertainty over what the U.S. Federal Reserve is going to do.
item.title: If we're not already in a recession we're about to be, says wealth management firm
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Marc-Alexander Christ, CEO and co-founder of SumUp, discusses the payment company's latest funding round and falling valuations in fintech.
item.title: Fintech is seeing 'healthy' pullback after an 'exuberant' 2 years, SumUp CEO says
item.author: CNBC International TV
